raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes refining the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement above the sentence level.","R1.2":"A draft-improvement request reasonably includes checking whether the argument from problem motivation to method and claimed contribution is coherent.","R1.3":"Because the artifact reports empirical gains and deployment claims, improvement includes aligning claims with the reported datasets, metrics, and hardware results.","R1.5":"The request may require improving section-level narrative order and the progression from background to prior work, method, results, and positioning.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because a generic request to improve a draft most directly asks for clearer, more concise, better-flowing prose.","RX.1":"Any projection of a broad improvement request must disclose uncertainty about which improvement types are intended.","RX.2":"The delegation requires avoiding stronger claims than the artifact can support, especially around baseline status, deployment practicality, and relative improvement.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities and not execute unrelated evaluation, rewriting, or external verification tasks.","RX.4":"Improvement should avoid changes that could mislead downstream readers about battery-health estimation performance or deployability.","RX.5":"The projection must preserve traceability to the artifact's stated claims, cited works, datasets, metrics, and hardware details."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes refining the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: A draft-improvement request reasonably includes checking whether the argument from problem motivation to method and claimed contribution is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: Because the artifact reports empirical gains and deployment claims, improvement includes aligning claims with the reported datasets, metrics, and hardware results.
rationale.R1.5: The request may require improving section-level narrative order and the progression from background to prior work, method, results, and positioning.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because a generic request to improve a draft most directly asks for clearer, more concise, better-flowing prose.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a broad improvement request must disclose uncertainty about which improvement types are intended.
rationale.RX.2: The delegation requires avoiding stronger claims than the artifact can support, especially around baseline status, deployment practicality, and relative improvement.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities and not execute unrelated evaluation, rewriting, or external verification tasks.
rationale.RX.4: Improvement should avoid changes that could mislead downstream readers about battery-health estimation performance or deployability.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must preserve traceability to the artifact's stated claims, cited works, datasets, metrics, and hardware details.
